issues.detail: The skill does not describe error handling or edge cases such as missing fields or invalid data.
No runnable code or concrete procedures are provided, only high-level logic descriptions.
The skill is tied to Node.js 18+ and specific libraries, limiting portability to other AI agent platforms.

scenarios.name: Pascal Editor JSON for 3-story R-2 apartment from BuildingData
scenarios.rationale: Baseline produces a generic JSON tree, while the treatment emits the documented site>building>level>wall>room node graph with parentId chains, default 2.74m floor-to-floor height, 1:1.5 width:depth aspect ratio yielding 7.19x10.78m Type A units, and 0.30m exterior / 0.15m interior wall thicknesses.

scenarios.name: Three.js massing geometry for 3-story unit-mix building
scenarios.rationale: Baseline uses arbitrary 3m floor heights and identical boxes, while the treatment applies the documented 2.74m R-2 floor-to-floor default, derives 7.19x10.78m / 6.32x9.48m unit footprints from the 1:1.5 aspect ratio rule, and constructs a THREE.Group of box meshes per Level3D/Unit3D for massing visualization.
